ddls:
CREATE TABLE "incident_types" (
  "incident_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident type */,
  "incident_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the incident type */,
  "incident_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the incident type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_subtypes" (
  "incident_subtype_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident subtype */,
  "incident_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident type the subtype belongs to */,
  "incident_subtype_name" TEXT /* Name of the incident subtype */,
  "incident_subtype_description" TEXT /* Description of the incident subtype */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_subtype_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_categories" (
  "incident_category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident category */,
  "incident_category_name" TEXT /* Name of the incident category */,
  "incident_category_description" TEXT /* Description of the incident category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_severity" (
  "incident_severity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident severity level */,
  "incident_severity_name" TEXT /* Name of the incident severity level */,
  "incident_severity_description" TEXT /* Description of the incident severity level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_severity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "officers" (
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each officer */,
  "officer_name" TEXT /* Name of the officer */,
  "officer_badge_number" INTEGER /* Badge number of the officer */,
  "officer_rank" TEXT /* Rank of the officer */,
  "officer_district_id" INTEGER /* ID of the district the officer belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_involvement" (
  "incident_involvement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident involvement */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident */,
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the officer involved */,
  "incident_involvement_role" TEXT /* Role of the officer in the incident (e.g., responding officer, investigating officer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_involvement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "user_role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the system (e.g., analyst, officer, administrator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_comments" (
  "incident_comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident comment */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident the comment belongs to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_date_time" TEXT /* Date and time the comment was made */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_comment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_files" (
  "incident_file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident file */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident the file belongs to */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the file (e.g., image, document) */,
  "file_data" TEXT /* Binary data of the file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_file_id")
)
